headline: FDI Into Malaysia Surges 23% to RM82 Billion
context: MIDA reported approved foreign direct investment of RM82.4 billion in 2025, up 23% from 2024, driven by semiconductor, data centre, and renewable energy projects primarily in Penang and Johor.

cards.big: RM82 billion in FDI sounds impressive — but only 38% translates into actual jobs within 3 years
cards.sub: Capital-intensive investments in chips and data centres create fewer jobs per ringgit than manufacturing

cards.big: Data centre investments hit RM42 billion but generate only 8,200 direct jobs at RM5.1 million per position
cards.sub: Compare to electronics manufacturing at RM180,000 per job — data centres are capital, not employment, plays

cards.big: 87% of FDI concentrated in Penang, Selangor, Johor — widening gap with East Malaysia and northern states
cards.sub: Kelantan, Terengganu, and Perlis received combined 1.8% of total FDI approvals

cards.big: MIDA's approved vs realised FDI ratio averaged only 52% over past 5 years — half of announcements never materialise
cards.sub: No penalty mechanism exists for companies that receive tax incentives but fail to invest as pledged

cards.big: Headline FDI figures are political PR — the real number is how many Malaysians actually get hired
Should FDI tax incentives be clawed back if companies fail to deliver 80% of promised jobs within 4 years?
